title: SACRAMENTO – Adding to her package of bills focused on solutions to homelessness and the first in a series of bills to be announced focused on housing access and affordability, tod
prompt_preview:
[1 chunks] Task: Extract all distinct claims about AI or emerging technology in this press release. Do NOT assume a fixed number.
Output JSON only matching the provided schema.
Return concise, standalone claim_texts that could be tested against scientific literature. Exclude purely procedural statem

claims.claim_text: One quarter of former foster youth experienced homelessness in California between the ages of 21-23.
Nearly one in three transition-age foster youth in California experiences homelessness.
Stable housing impacts virtually all aspects of life and is a critical social determinant of health.
AB 963 will expand the number of affordable housing units available to former foster youth struggling with housing security.
The essential need is adequate affordable housing dedicated to transition-aged foster youth.
California has both grant and tax credit programs that support affordable housing.
The opportunity exists to draw commercial debt and equity investments from the private and philanthropic sectors to develop and acquire affordable housing for transition-aged foster youth.
